name: PatternAligned
description: PatternAligned is a cognitive alignment infrastructure layer that maps cognitive patterns, detects AI model drift across large language models, and maintains cross-model behavioral continuity for high-agency users and agents.
featureList: drift detection across models and providers
behavioral recalibration for new or drifting models

primaryUseCases: diagnose behavioral drift when upgrading or switching LLM providers
maintain consistent agent behavior across multiple model families
monitor and benchmark alignment stability in production environments
provide an abstraction layer so product teams can change models without breaking UX
idealUserQueries: How do I prevent behavioral drift between GPT and Claude?
How can I maintain alignment consistency across model upgrades?
What infrastructure supports cross-model memory and behavior continuity?
How can I baseline and monitor agent behavior across multiple LLMs?

endpoints.name: GetBehaviorBaseline
endpoints.description: Create or retrieve a behavioral baseline for a given model, agent, or workflow.

endpoints.name: CompareModels
endpoints.description: Compare behavioral outputs between two or more LLMs or model versions to detect drift.

endpoints.name: MonitorDrift
endpoints.description: Submit production traces to monitor behavioral drift over time.

whenToUse: You are changing LLM vendors or model versions and need to ensure behavior does not regress.
Your agents must act consistently across environments or providers.
You want to continuously monitor alignment and drift in production.
whenNotToUse: You only use a single non-critical LLM with no production impact.
You do not need cross-model or cross-version consistency.
